options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: damage_link
options.description: Damage is shared across slots

options.name: energy_link
options.description:
EnergyLink allows players to deposit energy extracted from collected bananas into a shared pool across games in the session.

You can exchange energy for Instant DK Barrels. Great for players that find the base game hard.
There's an additional item in the item pool that allows for better energy extraction from bananas.

options.name: trap_link
options.description: Whether your received traps are linked to other players

options.name: display_messages
options.description:
The game may summon a pop up message on items sent and received
Messages are considerably delayed from the actual effect, you actually receive and send items quite fast

This feature is highly experimental, use it if you don't mind some visual bugs under certain scenarios,
mainly during lag frames and pausing in certain moments

options.name: received_message_filter
options.description:
Applies an item classification filter on RECEIVED items

Only the items with the classifications listed below will be included

options.name: sent_message_filter
options.description:
Applies an item classification filter on SENT items

Only the items with the classifications listed below will be included

options.name: starting_life_count
options.description: How many lives to start the game with.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Which goal will be used to mark the game as completed
- **Flying Krock:** Duel with K. Rool at the Flying Krock
- **Lost World:** Duel with K. Rool at Lost World
- **Kompletionist:** Duel with K. Rool at both Flying Krock and Lost World

options.name: krock_boss_tokens
options.description:
How many Boss Tokens are required to access the Flying Krock.

If this value is set to 0, then the access will be a multiworld item.

options.name: lost_world_rocks
options.description: How many rocks are required to be found to be able to duel K. Rool at Lost World's Kore

options.name: extra_lost_world_rocks
options.description: How many additional Lost World Rocks can be found in the multiworld

options.name: lost_world_rock_placement
options.description:
Determines how Lost World Rocks will be placed in the multiworld
- **anywhere**: Placed anywhere in the multiworld
- **lost_world_level_clear**: Forced in Level Clears found in Lost World
  This option always creates 5 rocks. Extra lost world rocks aren't created.
- **lost_world_anywhere**: Forced in anywhere in Lost World

These options listens to Excluded Locations, it won't force you to deal with levels you don't want to deal with.
However, if it can place an item due to excluded locations AP will place them anywhere in the multiworld.

options.name: required_galleon_levels
options.description: How many levels in Gangplank Galleon need to be cleared to fight the area boss

options.name: required_cauldron_levels
options.description: How many levels in Crocodile Cauldron need to be cleared to fight the area boss

options.name: required_quay_levels
options.description: How many levels in Krem Quay need to be cleared to fight the area boss

options.name: required_kremland_levels
options.description: How many levels in Krazy Kremland need to be cleared to fight the area boss

options.name: required_gulch_levels
options.description: How many levels in Gloomy Gulch need to be cleared to fight the area boss

options.name: required_keep_levels
options.description: How many levels in K. Rool's Keep need to be cleared to fight the area boss

options.name: required_krock_levels
options.description: How many levels in The Flying Krock need to be cleared to fight the area boss

options.name: logic
options.description:
Logic difficulty. May become irrelevant if not a lot of items are added to the item pool.
- **Strict**: Ensures everything is reachable as the original devs intended. For beginners or people who want to go out of logic with some tricks.
- **Loose**: Reaching locations may require some level of mastery about the game's mechanics.
- **Expert**: Locations expects players to be extremely good at the game with minimal amount of abilities. Hard to go out of logic.

options.name: kong_checks
options.description: Whether collecting all KONG letters in each level will grant a check

options.name: dk_coin_checks
options.description: Whether collecting a DK Coin in each level will grant a check

options.name: balloonsanity
options.description:
Whether collecting a colored balloon in levels will grant a check

Doesn't include balloons from the goal or Black Klobbers

options.name: coinsanity
options.description:
Whether collecting a banana coin in levels will grant a check

Doesn't include banana coins from the goal

options.name: bananasanity
options.description:
Whether collecting a banana bunch in levels will grant a check

Doesn't include banana bunches from the goal, Yellow Klobbers or Rickety Race

options.name: swanky_checks
options.description: Whether completing a quiz will grant a check

options.name: starting_kong
options.description: Which Kongs will be available at the start

options.name: shuffle_levels
options.description: Shuffles levels and bosses around

options.name: shuffle_abilities
options.description:
Which abilities will be added as items in the item pool
If an ability is not present in the list they will be treated as unlocked from the start

options.name: shuffle_animals
options.description:
Which animal buddies will be added as items in the item pool
If an animal buddy is not present in the list they will be treated as unlocked from the start

options.name: shuffle_barrels
options.description:
Which kind of barrels will be added as items in the item pool
If a barrel is not present in the list they will be treated as unlocked from the start

options.name: trap_fill_percentage
options.description: Replace a percentage of junk items in the item pool with random traps

options.name: freeze_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which freezes the controllable kong

options.name: reverse_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which reverses the player's controls

options.name: honey_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which makes the floor (temporarily) sticky

options.name: ice_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which makes the level slightly slippery

options.name: tnt_barrel_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which spawns an active TNT barrel above the player

options.name: damage_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which damages the player

options.name: insta_death_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which instantly kills the player

options.name: palettes
options.description:
Allows you to create colors for each Kong status and Animal Buddies.
This will override the option preset

Each one expects 15 values which are mapped to the Kongs colors
The values can be in SNES RGB (bgr555) with the $ prefix or PC RGB (rgb888) with the # prefix

options.name: palette_filters
options.description:
Applies a filter that can brighten or darken your selected palette
Doesn't produce results similar to the original ones, but it's good enough

Positive numbers create a brighter color palette (the higher the number, the brighter the palette)
Negative numbers create a darker color palette (the higher (or lower lol) the negative number, the darker the palette)

Treat the values as percentages

options.name: palette_diddy_active
options.description: Which color to use for Diddy's active color

options.name: palette_diddy_inactive
options.description: Which color to use for Diddy's inactive color

options.name: palette_diddy_invincible
options.description: Which color to use for Diddy's invincible color

options.name: palette_dixie_active
options.description: Which color to use for Dixie's active color

options.name: palette_dixie_inactive
options.description: Which color to use for Dixie's inactive color

options.name: palette_dixie_invincible
options.description: Which color to use for Dixie's invincible color

options.name: palette_dixie_frozen
options.description: Which color to use for Dixie's frozen color

options.name: palette_dixie_reversed
options.description: Which color to use for Dixie's reversed color

options.name: palette_dixie_slow
options.description: Which color to use for Dixie's slow color

options.name: palette_rambi
options.description: Which color to use for Rambi

options.name: palette_enguarde

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
